methods.description: This method involves the acquisition of extensive low-frequency ground-penetrating radar (GPR) data to study the sub-surface architecture in ice-proximal locations at Skeiðarársandur. The data helps in understanding the large-scale sediment architecture of sandur environments.

steps.description: Extensive (~40 km of along track measurements) low-frequency (40 and 100 MHz) ground-penetrating radar (GPR) data were acquired in ice-proximal locations at Skeiðarársandur.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire low-frequency GPR data along a 40 km track at Skeiðarársandur

steps.description: A 40 MHz UTSI Groundvue 7 (GV7) low-frequency ground-penetrating radar (GPR) system was used to collect radar profiles of the large-scale sub-surface architecture.
steps.searchable_summary: Use 40 MHz GV7 GPR system to collect radar profiles

steps.description: Coincident 100 MHz GPR profiles (using a separate GV7 system) were acquired at specific locations to provide higher resolution data.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire 100 MHz GPR profiles for higher resolution data
